input.headline: Food that could be ruining your sleep – and how to fix it

raw.headline: Food that could be ruining your sleep – and how to fix it

winner: I tried to eat my way to better sleep, but all I got was a midnight snack and a whole lot of regret — turns out, food that could be ruining your sleep is just a bedtime burrito away.

candidates.text: I tried eating a sandwich before bed, and now my dreams are just a never-ending commercial for bread. Turns out, the real sleep-ruiner isn't the cheese—it's the fact that I'm still hungry at 3 AM.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with relatable scenario, specific humor about dreams and hunger, concise and natural language."}

candidates.text: I tried eating a bedtime snack, but now I'm too wired to sleep — guess I'll just have to eat the alarm clock instead.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with the alarm clock, concise and specific to the topic, uses natural humor"}

candidates.text: I tried to fix my sleep issues by eating a literal "Bedtime Story," but now I’m just lying in the dark wondering why the protagonist in my stomach won't stop describing his feelings about the forest.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative and specific metaphor with a surprising twist, ties well to the headline topic, and uses natural English."}

candidates.text: I finally discovered that late-night pizza is what ruins my sleep, so now I only eat it during the day to ensure I can be perfectly exhausted by bedtime.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on a common problem, concise and specific with clever wordplay on 'perfectly exhausted'."}

candidates.text: I tried to eat a bowl of "sleepy" cereal to help me rest, but it turns out my insomnia is just a very aggressive case of "wakey" wake.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "Playful wordplay with 'sleepy' and 'wakey' adds surprise and specificity, aligns well with the headline topic, and is concise."}

candidates.text: I tried to eat my way to better sleep, but all I got was a midnight snack and a whole lot of regret — turns out, food that could be ruining your sleep is just a bedtime burrito away.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever and ties into the headline with a relatable scenario, but it lacks some punch and could be more specific about the food-sleep connection."}

candidates.text: Why did the tomato blush at night? It heard the salad say, "You're too *ketch* for me.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the pun on 'ketch' (related to ketchup), but it's a bit forced and not strongly connected to the headline about food affecting sleep. It lacks specificity to the topic and could be more surprising."}

candidates.text: Why did I stop eating coffee before bed? Because I finally figured out that it's *fixing* my sleep!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is clever with a twist but lacks punch and feels slightly forced in its wordplay."}

candidates.text: Why did the tomato go to bed angry? It couldn’t handle its nightshade.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is clever with the wordplay on 'nightshade,' but it's slightly generic and doesn't directly tie to the headline about food affecting sleep."}
